name: E3
epitaph: Once gaming's Super Bowl. Sony left first. Nintendo followed. COVID delivered the killing blow.

name: Casual Connect
epitaph: The go-to conference for the casual gaming boom. When mobile stopped being novel, so did it.

name: Spike VGAs
epitaph: Cringe-filled but beloved. Reincarnated by Geoff Keighley into something classier.

name: GDC Europe
epitaph: Cologne's developer conference. Quietly folded into gamescom's orbit.

name: Theranos
epitaph: $9 billion valuation built on a machine that didn't work. Elizabeth Holmes became a cautionary tale.

name: Quibi
epitaph: $1.75 billion raised. Six months alive. A premium mobile-only streaming service during a pandemic when everyone was home.

name: FTX
epitaph: Super Bowl ads, stadium naming rights, celebrity endorsements. Then the bank run. SBF is in prison.

name: Pets.com
epitaph: The sock puppet mascot outlived the company. A monument to the first bubble.

name: Webvan
epitaph: Online grocery delivery before anyone wanted it. Burned $800M building warehouses in 26 cities.

name: Vine
epitaph: Six-second videos changed internet comedy forever. Then Twitter killed it and TikTok ate the corpse.

name: Google+
epitaph: Google forced it on every user. They still didn't use it. A data breach provided the mercy kill.

name: MySpace
epitaph: Technically still online. Spiritually deceased. A generation's first social graph.

name: Blockbuster
epitaph: Turned down buying Netflix for $50M in 2000. Filed for bankruptcy 10 years later. One store remains in Bend, Oregon.

name: Toys R Us
epitaph: A generation's paradise strangled by leveraged buyout debt while Amazon ate its lunch.

name: Borders
epitaph: Outsourced online sales to Amazon. Dismissed ebooks. Watched Barnes & Noble pull ahead and never caught up.

name: Circuit City
epitaph: Laid off its highest-paid sales staff to cut costs. Sales collapsed. Best Buy feasted.

name: Lehman Brothers
epitaph: 158 years old. The bankruptcy that broke the global economy. Nobody was allowed to bail them out.

name: Bear Stearns
epitaph: Sold to JPMorgan for $2 a share. The first domino of the 2008 crisis.

name: Washington Mutual
epitaph: Largest bank failure in US history. Seized by the FDIC in September 2008.

name: Silicon Valley Bank
epitaph: 40 years of serving startups, undone in 48 hours when VCs tweeted each other into a panic.

name: Credit Suisse
epitaph: 167 years of Swiss banking. Swallowed by UBS in an emergency government-brokered sale.

name: Signature Bank
epitaph: Went down in the same week as SVB. Too much crypto exposure for regulators to stomach.

name: AAF
epitaph: The Alliance of American Football. Eight weeks of games. Collapsed before the first season ended.

name: USFL (original)
epitaph: Won an antitrust case against the NFL. The damages: three dollars. League folded.

name: XFL 1.0
epitaph: Vince McMahon's first try. One season of bad football and bad theater. $70 million lost.

name: XFL 2.0
epitaph: McMahon's second try. Five weeks in, the pandemic hit. Never recovered.

name: NFL Europe
epitaph: Once called the WLAF. Sixteen years of trying to sell American football to Europeans. They preferred football football.
